transcript: Hey, I was tryin’ to convert a… doorknob on my, uh… barn door, here.
This ol’ barn door’s crickety-crackin’ and I can’t… move the thing! I need to get some oil on there or a new door knob on here. You got a product I can use?
What type o’ so—? What is—? What sound is that?
It’s my barn door, my friend.
Sounds like you’re on a train.
This door is gonna fall off if I don’t get a new knob pronto.
Okay. The only knobs that we have in s—
…or like a double knob? Or triple knob? Ya know?
I wasn’t sure if you had the right product on hand, ya know, or not for a barn door.
We don’t really have anything for barn doors. Lot of our b— doors are for, um, a—
[delay] I wasn’t sure if you had the right product on hand, ya know, or not for a barn door. [pitch-shifted]
Sorry, we don’t really ha— W— Sorry, we don’t really have anything for barn doors.
A lot of doors— A lot of the knobs that we have are mainly just for regular doors. Um…—
Is there something that I can use, like, with mineral spirits to torta [sic] pour it on there and see—?
[creaking][delay] Is there something that I can use, like, with mineral spirits to torta [sic] pour it on there and see—? [pitch-shifted]
It’s kinda hard you with the— with that sound—
—goin’ back and forth. Is there a—?
Is there a way you can move—? Yeah, is there a way that you can, uh, like, go to a quieter place? Um—
Like I said, it’s hard to hear you.
I can quiet the thing down, you know, with mineral spirits or WD-40.
[creaking][delay] I can talk with the—I can quiet the thing down, you know, with mineral— [pitch-shifted]
Uh, do you have any mineral spirits on hand at all?—
Yeah, we sell m— Yeah, we sell mineral spirits.
I should get a whole case, I figure, because it’s—
It’s a big barn door—about 20 feet by 15 feet—
Sir, do you live in Brooklyn?
[delay, pitch-down] —It’s a big barn door—about 20 feet by fiftee—
Where d—? Where’re y’all located up ‘ere?—
[delay, pitch-down] Where d—? Where’re y’all located up ‘ere?
How much do y’all charge up ‘ere?
[delay, pitch-shifted] How much ya charge… up ‘ere?
[delay, pitch-up] How much do y’all charge up ‘ere?
Once again, it’s kinda hard to hear you.
I’m hearin’, like, three different things—that door.—
It—it’s a lotta noise. It’s making it hard to hear you.
Alright, sir, sir, do you—do you—do you—? Are—? Do you understanding what I’m saying?
It’s hard to hear you right now. Sir?
WD-4— Yeah, I’m trying to figure out the cost—
Sir, it’s hard to hear you. I can’t hear—
[creaking][delay, pitch-up, sped-up] WD-4— Yeah, I’m trying to figure out the cost—
understand or hear a word that you’re saying.
Just the price—how much the cost of the p—item.
[creaking][delay] Just the price—how much the cost of the p—item.—
Alright. Once again, I still ca— Ugh, sir, once again, I still cannot hear you.
Sir, is there a way that you can move away? Because—
Yeah, once again, sir, I can’t hear you.
Is there a way you can move away from where you are?
Or an alternate product to WD-40, if you don’t have that in… on hand.—
—to WD-40. If you don’t have that in… on hand.—
[delay] Or—Or an alternate product to WD-40, if you don’t have that in… on hand.—
Sir, it’s not that we don’t have it, it’s the fact that I cannot hear you!
Well I don’t even know the price—
What aisle is it in too? If I come up later.
Sir, I can’t hear you! I cannot hear you!
[creaking][delay] I don’t even know the price—What aisle is it in too? If I come up later.—
Do you—? Sir, I can’t hear you!—
I just need the aisle number. What aisle is it in?
[creaking][delay, pitch-down] I just need the aisle number—
[delay, pitch-down] What aisle is it in?—
I need you to move away from where you are so I can hear you better. I cannot hear you.
What aisle number is the WD-40 in?
Sir, I c— Once again, I can’t hear you.
Is there a way you can move away?
[creaking][delay, pitch-down] What aisle number is the WD-40 in?
I’m movin’ around in circles, here. I think I’m tryin’ to...—
[creaking][delay, pitch-shifted] I’m movin’ around in circles. I think I’m tryin’ to…—I can hear you fine.
move away from the door into a quieter place—
—so that I can hear you. ‘Cause I cannot understand a word with all the noise around you.
Plus, there’s a, uh, there’s another voice on the phone with you.
So I’m trying to figure out what exactly you’re tryin’ to say.—
Reference to, uh, your location, you said that you’re… in Williamsburg.
I can’t— Open—? You’re open ’til when?
[delay] Reference to, uh, location, you said that you’re… in Williamsburg.
Alright, sir, we’re located— Sir, we’re located on South 2nd and Roebling.
You’re open ’til what? I can’t tell my clock.—
It’s— You can look at the knobs there. You can see if we— We do have mineral spirits, we do have WD-40. Hopefully that helps.
There’s nothing I can give you right now ‘cause it’s— I’m having a hard time hearing you.
Hold on! You couldn’t hit the side of the barn with a door knob!
